license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.srred56
title: CRISPR Editing of Human iPS cells with RNPs using Nucleofection
author: Synthego

steps_raw.instruction: Culture iPS cells on Matrigel® Matrix-coated plates until they are semiconfluent.
Coat a new 6-well plate with Matrigel® Matrix and incubate according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
Aspirate Matrigel® Matrix from the plates after coating.
Fill each well with 2 ml mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK inhibitor. undetermined, mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK inhibitor
Incubate at 37°C until nucleofection. Undetermined, Incubation
Prepare 70 μl additional pre-warmed mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK per reaction for cell recovery after nucleofection. undetermined, mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK
Make sure that the entire Nucleofector TM Supplement is added to the Nucleofector TM Solution (according to manufacturer's protocol) and that the mixture is not more than 3 months old.
Hydrate sgRNA (3 nmol) in 30 μl 1X nuclease-free TE buffer to make 100 μM (100 pmol/μl) sgRNA. Concentration of Cas9 nuclease: 20 μM (20 pmol/μl). undetermined, 1X nuclease-free TE buffer
Mix the components following the proportions given in the table for a single reaction (scale up appropriately). a undetermined, sgRNA undetermined, Cas9
Incubate the RNPs at room temperature for 10 minutes, then place on ice until use. 00:10:00, Incubation
Prior to electroporation, detach the iPS cells from the Matrigel® Matrix plates by incubating with pre-warmed Accutase® for 3-5 minutes at 37°C. Undetermined, Incubation 00:03:00, Incubation
Dissociate the cells into a single cell suspension by pipetting the suspension carefully up and down 4-6 times.
Add the same amount of mTeSR1 medium to stop Accutase®.
Harvest the cells to an appropriately sized tube.
Count an aliquot of the detached cells and determine cell density.
Transfer the required number of cells (5 x 10 5 cells per reaction) to a Corning® 15 ml Centrifuge Tube.
Centrifuge cells at 115 x g for 3 minutes at room temperature. 00:03:00, Centrifugation
Aspirate the supernatant completely. The cell pellets will not be packed tightly so care is required when removing supernatant.
Add 5 ml of PBS buffer and wash cells by gently pipetting up and down. undetermined, PBS buffer
Centrifuge at 115 x g for 3 minutes at room temperature. 00:03:00, Centrifugation
Aspirate the supernatant.

Resuspend cells in Lonza P3 Nucleofector TM solution at 20 μl/reaction. Work quickly, but carefully, and avoid leaving cells in the Nucleofector TM solution for longer than 15 minutes. Avoid bubble formation.
Mix 20 μl of cells with 5 μl RNPs (prepared in Step 8) for each reaction in an appropriate tube/plate. undetermined, cells undetermined, RNPs
Transfer each cell-RNP solution (25 μl) to each well of the Nucleocuvette TM strip. Click the lid into place.
Gently tap the Nucleocuvette TM strip on the benchtop to make sure the sample covers the bottom of the cuvette and that there are no bubbles in the wells.

Place the Nucleocuvette TM strip with closed lid into the retainer of the 4D-X Core unit. Check for proper orientation of the Nucleocuvette TM strip. Larger cutout is the top (A1 and A2) and smaller cutout is the bottom (H1 and H2).

Use the electroporation protocol “CA137.” Press “Start” on the display of the core unit. After run completion, the screen should display a green “+” over the wells that were successfully transfected. Remove the NucleocuvetteTM strip from the core unit.
Use a multichannel pipette to add 70 μl pre-warmed mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK into each well of the Nucleocuvette TM strip to recover the cells. undetermined, pre-warmed mTeSR1 medium + 10 μM ROCK
Transfer cells from each well to the pre-warmed 6-well plate (prepared in Step 2) and incubate overnight in a humidified 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator. Undetermined, 5% CO2 incubator
Change with fresh mTeSR1 (without ROCK inhibitor) 24 hours after nucleofection and daily until time for cell harvest. 24:00:00
Incubate the cells for 4 days in a humidified 37°C/5% CO 2 incubator. Undetermined, 5% CO2 incubator 96:00:00, 5% CO2 incubator

Conduct analyses to determine editing efficiency: PCR, Sanger sequencing, and ICE analysis . Next-Gen Sequencing, FACS, or functional tests may be conducted as alternatives. Option : If storing cells for future use is desired, split cells into two groups (one for analysis and one for cell culture).
Extract DNA from cells.
